content:
The Angular ecosystem is expanding beyond the browser’s traditional limits! From running AI models locally to migrating your entire test suite to Vitest, our community experts are leading the charge into a more performant future.
Check out these latest deep dives:
Why the Frontend Should Run AI Models Locally with ONNX
Sonu Kapoor @SonuKapoor1978 explores a fascinating shift in architecture: bringing AI directly to the client. Learn how to run AI models locally using ONNX and Angular for better privacy and lower latency.

• Read the article: https://thenewstack.io/why-the-frontend-should-run-ai-models-locally-with-onnx/
Signal Forms: Smarter Inputs and Reactive Routing
In this real-world guide, Sonu Kapoor @SonuKapoor1978 breaks down how to use Angular Signals to create highly responsive inputs and manage complex routing states with ease.

• Check out the deep dive: https://www.codemag.com/Article/2511041/Angular-Signals-in-the-Real-World-Smarter-Inputs-and-Reactive-Routing
Angular Signals: A Recap of the Framework’s Evolution
Fanis Prodromou @prodromouf provides a comprehensive retrospective and look-ahead. See how far Signals have come and how they continue to redefine the core DNA of Angular development.

• Watch the video: https://youtu.be/MSGYnc83UJY
Signal Forms: Custom Controls (French)
Kevin Davila @kevindaviladev continues his expert series on the newest version of Angular. This installment focuses on building Custom Form Controls specifically designed for the Signal Forms API.

• Read the blog (French): https://www.codeabien.com/blog/angular-signals-recap
Vitest in Angular 21: What’s New and How to Migrate? (German & English)
Johannes Hoppe @JohannesHoppe provides the ultimate guide to switching your testing environment. Whether you are reading in German or English, learn why Vitest is the new standard and how to migrate your code samples.

• Read the German guide: https://angular-buch.com/blog/2025-11-zu-vitest-migrieren Read the English guide: https://angular.schule/blog/2025-11-migrate-to-vitest
Are you planning a migration to Vitest or experimenting with local AI? Your insights could help a fellow developer make the leap.
Keep the momentum going! Use #AngularSparkles and #Vitest to share your migration tips and project wins 👇
Local AI, Reactive Routing, and the Vitest Migration! ⚡ was originally published in Angular Blog on Medium, where people are continuing the conversation by highlighting and responding to this story.
